justification: Options FollowSymLinks or Indexes combined with AllowOverride All allows user-controlled .htaccess to escalate privileges and enable directory traversal or execution of arbitrary scripts.
Permissive Timeout + KeepAliveTimeout + unbounded MaxKeepAliveRequests create compounding resource exhaustion vectors. An attacker can hold connections open and multiply the DoS impact.
Both ServerTokens and ServerSignature leaking version details compound the information disclosure risk, giving attackers precise version targeting.
Enabling the status module with LoadModule and setting ServerTokens to Full reveals detailed server information, aiding attackers in reconnaissance and targeted exploitation.

Loading the status_module and setting User to root allows an attacker to access detailed server statistics via /server-status, which can be exploited for further reconnaissance or privilege escalation.

Enabling Options All and loading the status_module allows an attacker to access detailed server statistics via /server-status, which can be used for further reconnaissance or denial of service attacks.
Enabling Options Indexes and loading the autoindex_module allows directory listings to be displayed, which can expose sensitive file structures and facilitate further reconnaissance or exploitation.
Setting User and Group to root grants the Apache server full administrative privileges, allowing an attacker who gains access through any vulnerability to escalate their privileges immediately.
Overly permissive request size limits across line, fields, and field size create compounding HTTP request smuggling and buffer overflow risk when multiple limits are misconfigured together.
Setting ServerTokens to Full and SSLProtocol to All provides detailed information about the server's software version, which can be used by an attacker for targeted exploitation or reconnaissance.
WebDAV modules (dav_module, dav_fs_module) loaded with permissive AllowOverride enables remote file upload and execution. This chain has the highest amplification — remote code execution in one step.
